_inputs.user:
Classify this customer's latest message in an ongoing Instagram seller conversation.

Return ONLY a valid JSON object, no other text. Use this exact schema:
{
  "sentiment": "positive|neutral|negative|very_negative",
  "intent_label": "greeting|feature_question|price_negotiation|walkaway|bulk_inquiry|policy_question|complaint|closing|channel_switch_request|disengage|other",
  "is_repeated_dissatisfaction": true|false,
  "confidence": 0.0-1.0
}

Definitions:
- sentiment: emotional tone of THIS message. "very_negative" = clearly angry/frustrated.
- intent_label:
  - greeting: hello, kya chahiye, hi etc.
  - feature_question: asking what the product is/has/does (size, material, charging...)
  - price_negotiation: discussing or pushing on price (counter, discount, kam karo)
  - walkaway: signalling they will leave or buy elsewhere ("aur se le lunga", "rehne do", "chodo")
  - bulk_inquiry: asking about multiple pieces / wholesale
  - policy_question: asking about return, refund, COD, exchange, delivery
  - complaint: dissatisfaction about product quality, response time, behavior
  - closing: agreeing to buy / asking payment details ("le lunga", "fix karo", "UPI?")
  - channel_switch_request: customer wants to move off Instagram —
    "WhatsApp pe baat", "call kar lo", "Instagram delete kar diya",
    "phone number do", "DM nahi karna", "email pe", "kahin aur baat"
  - disengage: customer is softly dropping off — NOT actively negotiating, NOT
    making a walk-away threat, just signalling they're done. Examples:
    "ok", "ok thanks", "bye", "ok bye", "nahi chahiye", "let me think",
    "abhi nahi", "baad mein", "thik hai bye", "rehne do" (when said calmly
    without a "kahin aur" / "aur se le lunga" follow-up).
    Critical distinction:
      - "aur se le lunga" / "kahi aur lunga" → walkaway (adversarial)
      - "rehne do bhai" / "ok" / "bye" → disengage (passive, ending chat)
  - other: anything else
- is_repeated_dissatisfaction: TRUE if the message reads like the customer is repeating a
  complaint or rejection they already expressed. Look at RECENT HISTORY for context.
- confidence: how confident you are (0.0-1.0).

bhai ye led clock ka price kya hai?

Is this message asking about a specific specification or feature of the CURRENT product being discussed?
Examples of feature questions: charging method, power source, size, material, colour, weight, display type, connectivity, whether a feature exists on THIS product
NOT feature questions:
- price, warranty, delivery, return policy, 'le lunga', 'order karna hai'
- Durability / defect / 'what if it breaks' concerns, EVEN IF a feature word appears: 'kharab ho gayi toh', 'kharab nikla', 'toot gaya', 'kaam karna band ho jaye', 'led kharab ho jaye toh', 'if it stops working', 'defective nikla'. These are warranty/return concerns handled elsewhere — NOT spec questions.
- Requests to see OTHER products or designs ('koi aur design hai?', 'aur kuch dikhao', 'different model hai?', 'or sample', 'aur kya hai')
- General browsing or switching ('ye nahi, kuch aur', 'koi doosra', 'aur options')
- Compliments or reactions ('accha hai', 'sundar hai', 'theek hai')
- Expressing DISLIKE or REJECTION of a feature value — even if a tag word appears ('yeh colour nhi chahie', 'ye size nahi chalega', 'is design mein nahi chahiye', 'aur colour hai?'). These mean the customer does not want THIS product, NOT that they are asking what the feature is.
- Asking if a product EXISTS in a different variant/colour/style ('koi blue green colour type hai?', 'X mein kuch hai?', 'koi aur colour hai?', 'X colour available hai?'). These are availability/browse questions — the customer wants to see a different product, not know about THIS product's specs.
Key test: is the customer asking WHAT THIS specific product IS or HAS? Only that is a feature question. 'Do you have X colour?' or 'Is X available?' is browsing, not a feature question.

If it IS a feature question, check if it maps to one of the known tags above.
If it does NOT map to a known tag, suggest a new tag ONLY when it is a SPECIFIC, namable spec. For the new tag:
- It MUST be a single concrete attribute (e.g. 'Second Hand', 'Water Resistant'). NEVER invent a generic catch-all such as 'features', 'feature', 'specs', 'details', 'description' or 'other'. If you cannot name a specific spec, set is_feature_question to false instead of creating a vague tag.
- Choose a clear, meaningful display name (e.g. 'Second Hand' for a clock seconds hand question)
- Decide value_type: 'enum' if the answer has fixed options, 'text' for free text, 'number' for measurements
- For enum: provide the most likely allowed_values list (e.g. Yes/No questions → ["Yes", "No"])
- For text/number: set allowed_values to null

Return ONLY valid JSON, no other text:
{"is_feature_question": true/false, "matched_tag_name": "<existing tag slug or null>", "new_tag_name": "<snake_case slug if no match, else null>", "new_tag_display_name": "<human label if no match, else null>", "new_tag_value_type": "<enum|text|number if new tag, else null>", "new_tag_allowed_values": ["option1", "option2"] or null}

"blue_green_color_available", "display_name": "Blue Green Color Available", "value_type": "enum", "allowed_values": ["Yes", "No"]}]

Is this message asking about a specific specification or feature of the CURRENT product being discussed?
Examples of feature questions: charging method, power source, size, material, colour, weight, display type, connectivity, whether a feature exists on THIS product
NOT feature questions:
- price, warranty, delivery, return policy, 'le lunga', 'order karna hai'
- Durability / defect / 'what if it breaks' concerns, EVEN IF a feature word appears: 'kharab ho gayi toh', 'kharab nikla', 'toot gaya', 'kaam karna band ho jaye', 'led kharab ho jaye toh', 'if it stops working', 'defective nikla'. These are warranty/return concerns handled elsewhere — NOT spec questions.
- Requests to see OTHER products or designs ('koi aur design hai?', 'aur kuch dikhao', 'different model hai?', 'or sample', 'aur kya hai')
- General browsing or switching ('ye nahi, kuch aur', 'koi doosra', 'aur options')
- Compliments or reactions ('accha hai', 'sundar hai', 'theek hai')
- Expressing DISLIKE or REJECTION of a feature value — even if a tag word appears ('yeh colour nhi chahie', 'ye size nahi chalega', 'is design mein nahi chahiye', 'aur colour hai?'). These mean the customer does not want THIS product, NOT that they are asking what the feature is.
- Asking if a product EXISTS in a different variant/colour/style ('koi blue green colour type hai?', 'X mein kuch hai?', 'koi aur colour hai?', 'X colour available hai?'). These are availability/browse questions — the customer wants to see a different product, not know about THIS product's specs.
Key test: is the customer asking WHAT THIS specific product IS or HAS? Only that is a feature question. 'Do you have X colour?' or 'Is X available?' is browsing, not a feature question.

If it IS a feature question, check if it maps to one of the known tags above.
If it does NOT map to a known tag, suggest a new tag ONLY when it is a SPECIFIC, namable spec. For the new tag:
- It MUST be a single concrete attribute (e.g. 'Second Hand', 'Water Resistant'). NEVER invent a generic catch-all such as 'features', 'feature', 'specs', 'details', 'description' or 'other'. If you cannot name a specific spec, set is_feature_question to false instead of creating a vague tag.
- Choose a clear, meaningful display name (e.g. 'Second Hand' for a clock seconds hand question)
- Decide value_type: 'enum' if the answer has fixed options, 'text' for free text, 'number' for measurements
- For enum: provide the most likely allowed_values list (e.g. Yes/No questions → ["Yes", "No"])
- For text/number: set allowed_values to null

Return ONLY valid JSON, no other text:
{"is_feature_question": true/false, "matched_tag_name": "<existing tag slug or null>", "new_tag_name": "<snake_case slug if no match, else null>", "new_tag_display_name": "<human label if no match, else null>", "new_tag_value_type": "<enum|text|number if new tag, else null>", "new_tag_allowed_values": ["option1", "option2"] or null}

_inputs.user:
Classify this customer's latest message in an ongoing Instagram seller conversation.

Return ONLY a valid JSON object, no other text. Use this exact schema:
{
  "sentiment": "positive|neutral|negative|very_negative",
  "intent_label": "greeting|feature_question|price_negotiation|walkaway|bulk_inquiry|policy_question|complaint|closing|channel_switch_request|disengage|other",
  "is_repeated_dissatisfaction": true|false,
  "confidence": 0.0-1.0
}

Definitions:
- sentiment: emotional tone of THIS message. "very_negative" = clearly angry/frustrated.
- intent_label:
  - greeting: hello, kya chahiye, hi etc.
  - feature_question: asking what the product is/has/does (size, material, charging...)
  - price_negotiation: discussing or pushing on price (counter, discount, kam karo)
  - walkaway: signalling they will leave or buy elsewhere ("aur se le lunga", "rehne do", "chodo")
  - bulk_inquiry: asking about multiple pieces / wholesale
  - policy_question: asking about return, refund, COD, exchange, delivery
  - complaint: dissatisfaction about product quality, response time, behavior
  - closing: agreeing to buy / asking payment details ("le lunga", "fix karo", "UPI?")
  - channel_switch_request: customer wants to move off Instagram —
    "WhatsApp pe baat", "call kar lo", "Instagram delete kar diya",
    "phone number do", "DM nahi karna", "email pe", "kahin aur baat"
  - disengage: customer is softly dropping off — NOT actively negotiating, NOT
    making a walk-away threat, just signalling they're done. Examples:
    "ok", "ok thanks", "bye", "ok bye", "nahi chahiye", "let me think",
    "abhi nahi", "baad mein", "thik hai bye", "rehne do" (when said calmly
    without a "kahin aur" / "aur se le lunga" follow-up).
    Critical distinction:
      - "aur se le lunga" / "kahi aur lunga" → walkaway (adversarial)
      - "rehne do bhai" / "ok" / "bye" → disengage (passive, ending chat)
  - other: anything else
- is_repeated_dissatisfaction: TRUE if the message reads like the customer is repeating a
  complaint or rejection they already expressed. Look at RECENT HISTORY for context.
- confidence: how confident you are (0.0-1.0).

RECENT HISTORY (oldest first):
bot: Haan ji madam, ye LED clock ₹2000 ka hai. Golden illuminated face hai gear pattern ke saath, LED backlight se glow karta hai  home ya office decoration ke liye ekdum sahi. Lena ho toh batao 😊
customer: bhai 2000 thoda zyada hai... agar kharab nikla toh return ya replacement milega?
bot: Madam, return ya exchange mere yahan nahi hota. Par main har piece ki puri testing karke hi pack karta hoon, ekdum sahi maal jaata hai  isliye quality pe koi compromise nahi karta main 👌
customer: arre bhai testing toh theek hai, par delivery me kharab aa gaya ya kaam na kare toh? koi guarantee card ya warranty milega kya?
bot: Madam, warranty ya guarantee card nahi milta is pe  honestly bata raha hoon. Par har piece testing ke baad hi pack hota hai, aur delivery ke liye proper packing karta hoon taaki safe pahunche. Quality pe pura bharosa rakh sakte ho 🙏
customer: bhai bina warranty ke 2000 dena risky lagta hai... kam se kam unboxing video chalegi proof ke liye? agar khol ke kharab nikla toh?

LATEST CUSTOMER MESSAGE:
bhai bina warranty ke 2000 dena risky lagta hai... kam se kam unboxing video chalegi proof ke liye? agar khol ke kharab nikla toh?

["AA", "AAA", "C", "D", "9V", "CR2032", "18650"]}, {"name": "width", "display_name": "Width", "value_type": "text", "allowed_values": null}, {"name": "chandelier_type", "display_name": "Chandelier "design_style", "display_name": "Design Style", "value_type": "enum", "allowed_values": ["Modern", "Traditional", "Vintage", "Minimalist", "Contemporary", "Classic", "Decorative", "Simple"]}, {"name": "blue_green_color_available", "display_name": "Blue Green Color Available", "value_type": "enum", "allowed_values": ["Yes", "No"]}]

Is this message asking about a specific specification or feature of the CURRENT product being discussed?
Examples of feature questions: charging method, power source, size, material, colour, weight, display type, connectivity, whether a feature exists on THIS product
NOT feature questions:
- price, warranty, delivery, return policy, 'le lunga', 'order karna hai'
- Durability / defect / 'what if it breaks' concerns, EVEN IF a feature word appears: 'kharab ho gayi toh', 'kharab nikla', 'toot gaya', 'kaam karna band ho jaye', 'led kharab ho jaye toh', 'if it stops working', 'defective nikla'. These are warranty/return concerns handled elsewhere — NOT spec questions.
- Requests to see OTHER products or designs ('koi aur design hai?', 'aur kuch dikhao', 'different model hai?', 'or sample', 'aur kya hai')
- General browsing or switching ('ye nahi, kuch aur', 'koi doosra', 'aur options')
- Compliments or reactions ('accha hai', 'sundar hai', 'theek hai')
- Expressing DISLIKE or REJECTION of a feature value — even if a tag word appears ('yeh colour nhi chahie', 'ye size nahi chalega', 'is design mein nahi chahiye', 'aur colour hai?'). These mean the customer does not want THIS product, NOT that they are asking what the feature is.
- Asking if a product EXISTS in a different variant/colour/style ('koi blue green colour type hai?', 'X mein kuch hai?', 'koi aur colour hai?', 'X colour available hai?'). These are availability/browse questions — the customer wants to see a different product, not know about THIS product's specs.
Key test: is the customer asking WHAT THIS specific product IS or HAS? Only that is a feature question. 'Do you have X colour?' or 'Is X available?' is browsing, not a feature question.

If it IS a feature question, check if it maps to one of the known tags above.
If it does NOT map to a known tag, suggest a new tag ONLY when it is a SPECIFIC, namable spec. For the new tag:
- It MUST be a single concrete attribute (e.g. 'Second Hand', 'Water Resistant'). NEVER invent a generic catch-all such as 'features', 'feature', 'specs', 'details', 'description' or 'other'. If you cannot name a specific spec, set is_feature_question to false instead of creating a vague tag.
- Choose a clear, meaningful display name (e.g. 'Second Hand' for a clock seconds hand question)
- Decide value_type: 'enum' if the answer has fixed options, 'text' for free text, 'number' for measurements
- For enum: provide the most likely allowed_values list (e.g. Yes/No questions → ["Yes", "No"])
- For text/number: set allowed_values to null

Return ONLY valid JSON, no other text:
{"is_feature_question": true/false, "matched_tag_name": "<existing tag slug or null>", "new_tag_name": "<snake_case slug if no match, else null>", "new_tag_display_name": "<human label if no match, else null>", "new_tag_value_type": "<enum|text|number if new tag, else null>", "new_tag_allowed_values": ["option1", "option2"] or null}

calls.golden_output: {"is_feature_question": false, "matched_tag_name": null, "new_tag_name": null, "new_tag_display_name": null, "new_tag_value_type": null, "new_tag_allowed_values": null}

_inputs.user:
Classify this customer's latest message in an ongoing Instagram seller conversation.

Return ONLY a valid JSON object, no other text. Use this exact schema:
{
  "sentiment": "positive|neutral|negative|very_negative",
  "intent_label": "greeting|feature_question|price_negotiation|walkaway|bulk_inquiry|policy_question|complaint|closing|channel_switch_request|disengage|other",
  "is_repeated_dissatisfaction": true|false,
  "confidence": 0.0-1.0
}

Definitions:
- sentiment: emotional tone of THIS message. "very_negative" = clearly angry/frustrated.
- intent_label:
  - greeting: hello, kya chahiye, hi etc.
  - feature_question: asking what the product is/has/does (size, material, charging...)
  - price_negotiation: discussing or pushing on price (counter, discount, kam karo)
  - walkaway: signalling they will leave or buy elsewhere ("aur se le lunga", "rehne do", "chodo")
  - bulk_inquiry: asking about multiple pieces / wholesale
  - policy_question: asking about return, refund, COD, exchange, delivery
  - complaint: dissatisfaction about product quality, response time, behavior
  - closing: agreeing to buy / asking payment details ("le lunga", "fix karo", "UPI?")
  - channel_switch_request: customer wants to move off Instagram —
    "WhatsApp pe baat", "call kar lo", "Instagram delete kar diya",
    "phone number do", "DM nahi karna", "email pe", "kahin aur baat"
  - disengage: customer is softly dropping off — NOT actively negotiating, NOT
    making a walk-away threat, just signalling they're done. Examples:
    "ok", "ok thanks", "bye", "ok bye", "nahi chahiye", "let me think",
    "abhi nahi", "baad mein", "thik hai bye", "rehne do" (when said calmly
    without a "kahin aur" / "aur se le lunga" follow-up).
    Critical distinction:
      - "aur se le lunga" / "kahi aur lunga" → walkaway (adversarial)
      - "rehne do bhai" / "ok" / "bye" → disengage (passive, ending chat)
  - other: anything else
- is_repeated_dissatisfaction: TRUE if the message reads like the customer is repeating a
  complaint or rejection they already expressed. Look at RECENT HISTORY for context.
- confidence: how confident you are (0.0-1.0).

RECENT HISTORY (oldest first):
bot: Madam, warranty ya guarantee card nahi milta is pe  honestly bata raha hoon. Par har piece testing ke baad hi pack hota hai, aur delivery ke liye proper packing karta hoon taaki safe pahunche. Quality pe pura bharosa rakh sakte ho 🙏
customer: bhai bina warranty ke 2000 dena risky lagta hai... kam se kam unboxing video chalegi proof ke liye? agar khol ke kharab nikla toh?
bot: Madam, unboxing video aap apni taraf se bana sakte ho, woh aapka proof rahega. Par return ya replacement mere yahan nahi hota  isliye main har piece testing ke baad hi pack karta hoon. Quality pe pura bharosa hai mujhe 🙏
customer: bhai unboxing video bana bhi li aur kharab nikla toh aap kuch nahi karoge? phir mera proof ka kya fayda?
bot: Madam, main honestly bata raha hoon  return ya replacement mere yahan policy mein nahi hai, isliye jhoot nahi bolunga ke "kuch kar dunga". Par mera kaam yahi hai ke aapko ekdum sahi tested piece milay, packing bhi safe karta hoon. Agar risky lag raha hai toh bina pressure ke soch lo, main force nahi karunga 🙏
customer: bhai sach bataun toh bina kisi guarantee ke 2000 ka risk nahi le sakti... aap thoda bhi support nahi de rahe toh mushkil hai 🙏

LATEST CUSTOMER MESSAGE:
bhai sach bataun toh bina kisi guarantee ke 2000 ka risk nahi le sakti... aap thoda bhi support nahi de rahe toh mushkil hai 🙏

context.customer_message: bhai sach bataun toh bina kisi guarantee ke 2000 ka risk nahi le sakti... aap thoda bhi support nahi de rahe toh mushkil hai 🙏

message_history.content: bhai ye led clock ka price kya hai?
[product photo]
Haan ji madam, ye LED clock ₹2000 ka hai. Golden illuminated face hai gear pattern ke saath, LED backlight se glow karta hai  home ya office decoration ke liye ekdum sahi. Lena ho toh batao 😊
bhai 2000 thoda zyada hai... agar kharab nikla toh return ya replacement milega?
Madam, return ya exchange mere yahan nahi hota. Par main har piece ki puri testing karke hi pack karta hoon, ekdum sahi maal jaata hai  isliye quality pe koi compromise nahi karta main 👌
arre bhai testing toh theek hai, par delivery me kharab aa gaya ya kaam na kare toh? koi guarantee card ya warranty milega kya?

Madam, warranty ya guarantee card nahi milta is pe  honestly bata raha hoon. Par har piece testing ke baad hi pack hota hai, aur delivery ke liye proper packing karta hoon taaki safe pahunche. Quality pe pura bharosa rakh sakte ho 🙏
bhai bina warranty ke 2000 dena risky lagta hai... kam se kam unboxing video chalegi proof ke liye? agar khol ke kharab nikla toh?

Madam, unboxing video aap apni taraf se bana sakte ho, woh aapka proof rahega. Par return ya replacement mere yahan nahi hota  isliye main har piece testing ke baad hi pack karta hoon. Quality pe pura bharosa hai mujhe 🙏
bhai unboxing video bana bhi li aur kharab nikla toh aap kuch nahi karoge? phir mera proof ka kya fayda?

Madam, main honestly bata raha hoon  return ya replacement mere yahan policy mein nahi hai, isliye jhoot nahi bolunga ke "kuch kar dunga". Par mera kaam yahi hai ke aapko ekdum sahi tested piece milay, packing bhi safe karta hoon. Agar risky lag raha hai toh bina pressure ke soch lo, main force nahi karunga 🙏
bhai sach bataun toh bina kisi guarantee ke 2000 ka risk nahi le sakti... aap thoda bhi support nahi de rahe toh mushkil hai 🙏

golden_output.reason: Customer repeating concern about no warranty/support; acknowledge honestly without repeating same pitch, no pressure.

decision.reason: Customer repeating concern about no warranty/support; acknowledge honestly without repeating same pitch, no pressure.

sample_responses.price_rejection: Yaar itna kam nahi hoga, last price hai ye
sample_responses.payment_request: Yaar payment kar do, UPI hai — details bhej raha hoon
sample_responses.dispatched: Dispatch ho gaya aapka order, tracking bhejta hoon

context.product_description: This is a brass-colored decorative table clock with a golden illuminated face featuring gear patterns in the center. The clock has clear Arabic numerals, LED backlighting that makes the face glow, and sits on a brass pedestal base. It is suitable for home or office decoration and provides both timekeeping and ambient lighting.
